ticket_goal: UX: Reorganize Settings Page with Tabbed Interface

TECHNICAL SPECS.Summary: Reorganize the current Settings page into a clean, URL-driven, tabbed interface to support scalability as more features are added.
TECHNICAL SPECS.Strategic Importance: As the application grows, the Settings page has become cluttered. Grouping by functionality improves discoverability, reduces cognitive load, and prepares the UI for future extensions (e.g., additional AI providers, storage backends).
TECHNICAL SPECS.Advocate Perspective: Users benefit from a clear separation of concerns. URL-based tabs (?tab=...) allow for deep linking, improving UX for support and onboarding. Search functionality in the Template Manager is a high-value win for users with many snippets.
TECHNICAL SPECS.Skeptic Perspective: Introducing URL-based state management adds complexity to navigation. We must ensure state persists correctly during back/forward navigation. Separating BYOK and AI into two distinct tabs (or sub-tabs) prevents the BYOK/AI tab from becoming another 'junk drawer'.

Production Readiness.Logging: Log tab navigation events to identify most-used settings.
Production Readiness.Caching: N/A - Settings are mostly user-specific and dynamic.
Production Readiness.Rate Limiting: Ensure API routes powering these settings (e.g., disconnecting accounts) are properly rate-limited.

expected_output.State: Defined tab structure and URL-driven state plan.
expected_output.Actions: Ready for dev-agent to implement tab state (using searchParams) and refactor the Settings page.

fixes_applied: Reorganized settings page into URL-driven tabs (?tab=...)
Implemented tab navigation and active state management using useSearchParams and router.push
Added search functionality to TemplateManager using useMemo
Updated test suite imports to match file reorganization
Resolved hydration issues by wrapping page in Suspense
